name: Poro 34B
archDisplay: Dense transformer, BLOOM architecture + ALiBi (54 layers, d_model 7168, 56 heads)
note: Fully open Apache-2.0 Finnish/English/code model trained on 1T tokens on the LUMI supercomputer; pivotal European low-resource open LLM.
tpsNote: Q4 GGUF (~20GB) fits on a single 24GB RTX 4090 with limited headroom for context.

results.notes: Finnish FIN-bench average; FinGPT 13B scored 48.92 for comparison
HumanEval (Python, pass@10) per arXiv:2404.01856 Table 8; corrected from 41.8, a prior value that was not a pure HumanEval score.

MBPP (Python), pass@10 via the Bigcode Evaluation Harness. Re-verified 2026-07-16 directly against the live HTML of Table 8 (Appendix A.2, arXiv:2404.01856v3): 'MBPP' is its own standalone table row (47.40), fully distinct from the 'HumanEval' row (37.20) and from the separate 'Average' row (41.80, which is the mean of the two per-benchmark rows). 47.40 is NOT itself a composite/average score -- the prior note's phrase 'the two together form...the...Average' was explaining how the paper derives that separate Code Average metric, not asserting this MBPP value is averaged. Comparability caveat: the paper uses pass@10 rather than the pass@1 methodology most other corpus entries under this slug use, so direct cross-model comparison should account for that.

verificationNotes:
Fully confirmed by fetching the actual arXiv PDF (2404.01856, Luukkonen et al., "Poro 34B and the Blessing of Multilinguality") and the HF model card. Architecture (54 layers, d_model 7168, 56 heads, BLOOM+ALiBi), 34.2B params, Apache-2.0 license, and 2048 sequence length all match Table 5 of the paper exactly. All 8 benchmark rows verified literally against Tables 6, 7, and 8 of the paper, including the FinGPT-13B=48.92 comparison note and the HumanEval/MBPP average (37.20/47.40 → 41.80).

2026-07-16: Re-checked the mbpp=47.4 comparability flag by fetching the live HTML of arXiv:2404.01856v3 (Table 8, Appendix A.2) directly. HumanEval (37.20) and MBPP (47.40) are each their own distinct table row/cell; a separate 'Average' row (41.80) is the mean of those two per-benchmark rows and is not itself stored under any slug in this file. The flag was a false positive triggered by the prior note's incidental phrase 'form the...Average' (describing that separate metric), not by mbpp=47.4 being composite -- the score is confirmed as the genuine, standalone MBPP pass@10 figure. Notes on the mbpp entry were rewritten to remove the ambiguous phrasing and to flag the pass@10 (vs. pass@1) methodology as a comparability caveat for cross-model use; score, slug, and source were left unchanged.
